name: 1. Human sets up a new repo with repo.box
steps: Human has an existing git repo with code
Runs `git repobox init` → creates .repobox.yml template, sets gpg.program = repobox
Runs `git repobox keys generate --alias alice` → creates EVM key pair, registers alias alice
Sets `git config --local user.signingkey evm:0xAAA...123`
Edits .repobox.yml: creates founders group with their address
Adds permission rules: founders edit everything, founders merge >main
Commits and pushes → first signed commit in the repo, shown as alice

name: 2. Human onboards an AI agent with scoped permissions
steps: Human generates a key for the agent: `git repobox keys generate --alias claude` → claude (evm:0xBBB...456)
Edits .repobox.yml: adds evm:0xBBB...456 to agents group
Adds rules: agents push >feature/*, agents create >feature/*, agents append .repobox.yml
Commits the .repobox.yml change (human is founders, has edit permission)
Spawns the agent with: GIT_CONFIG_KEY_0=user.signingkey GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=evm:0xBBB...456
Agent clones the repo, creates feature/fix, makes changes, commits, pushes
Every commit is signed and shows as claude. Agent cannot touch main.

name: 3. Agent spawns a sub-agent on a feature branch
steps: Agent (claude) is working on feature/big-refactor
Agent generates a key: `git repobox keys generate --alias claude+roudy-piglet` → claude+roudy-piglet (evm:0xCCC...789)
Agent appends a direct permission rule to .repobox.yml: `evm:0xCCC...789 write >feature/big-refactor/*`
Commits the .repobox.yml change → ✅ (agent has append permission for .repobox.yml on feature branch)
Spawns sub-agent with GIT_CONFIG_VALUE_0=evm:0xCCC...789
Sub-agent works on the same feature branch, commits shown as claude+roudy-piglet
Work done. Agent reverts .repobox.yml changes (removes the permission line)
Agent merges feature/big-refactor into main → ✅ (no .repobox.yml changes in diff)
If agent forgot to revert: merge blocked with clear error about .repobox.yml changes
notes: Append = add lines at end of file only. Agent can't modify groups (they're above permissions). Instead, agent appends a direct identity rule at the bottom — lowest priority, scoped to the feature branch. Sub-agent alias uses plus notation: parent+childname.

name: 4. Agent tries to exceed its permissions
steps: Agent (claude) is in agents group, can push to >feature/* only
Agent tries: git checkout main → ✅ (read-only, passthrough)
Agent tries: git checkout -b release/v1 → ❌ no create permission for >release/*
Agent tries: git push origin main → ❌ no push permission for >main
Agent tries: git merge feature/x into main → ❌ main's .repobox.yml blocks this
Agent tries to edit .repobox.yml on main → ❌ only founders can edit .repobox.yml on main
Every rejection uses aliases: '❌ claude cannot edit .repobox.yml on >main'

name: 5. Founder reviews and merges agent's work
steps: Agent pushed commits to feature/fix (all signed, shown as claude)
Founder runs: git log --show-signature feature/fix → sees all commits verified by claude
Founder runs: git checkout main && git merge feature/fix
Shim checks: is alice allowed to merge into >main? → ✅
Shim checks: does the merge contain .repobox.yml changes? → No (agent reverted them)
Merge succeeds. main now has the agent's code, every commit traceable.

name: 6. Audit trail: who did what
steps: Run: git log --show-signature
Every commit shows the alias + EVM address that signed it
Founder commits: signed by alice (evm:0xAAA...123)
Agent commits: signed by claude (evm:0xBBB...456)
Sub-agent commits: signed by claude+roudy-piglet (evm:0xCCC...789)
Lineage is immediately visible: claude+roudy-piglet was spawned by claude
Full accountability: no unsigned commits possible when shim is active

name: 7. Agent pushes to main — file-scoped access
steps: Founder sets up @deploy-bot with narrow file permissions on main:
Rule: `@deploy-bot write >main` scoped to `k8s/**` and `CHANGELOG.md`
@deploy-bot pushes a commit that only touches k8s/deploy.yaml → ✅ allowed
@deploy-bot pushes a commit that only touches CHANGELOG.md → ✅ allowed
@deploy-bot pushes a commit touching k8s/deploy.yaml AND src/app.rs → ❌ blocked (src/app.rs not in scope)
Error: '❌ @deploy-bot cannot edit src/app.rs on >main'
All files in the commit must pass — one unauthorized file blocks the whole push
notes: Key principle: push permission + file scope = surgical access to protected branches. Every file in the diff is checked independently.
